title: When RAG beats fine-tuning
description: RAG injects fresh, citable knowledge at query time; fine-tuning bakes patterns into weights but cannot cite or update cheaply.
body: Use when deciding how to give a model domain knowledge. RAG retrieves passages at query time so knowledge stays current, is attributable via citations, and updates by changing the corpus; fine-tuning adjusts weights to learn style or format but cannot cite sources, is costly to update, and risks memorizing stale facts. Do not fine-tune to add factual knowledge that changes, and avoid expecting RAG to teach a new output format. Actions: use RAG for changing, citable facts and fine-tuning for stable behavior/format. Validation signals include a test that updated corpus content changes the answer without retraining. Risk boundary: relying on baked-in weights for facts that change produces confident stale answers; prefer retrieval with citations.
